tossups.question: In one novel, this character travels to South Africa, assassinates his daughter’s elderly guardian, organizes the lynching of the Black man Jupiter, and then releases hundreds of plague-ridden rats on a ship. John Ashbury’s introduction to a Penguin Classic about this character notes memorable scenes involving him battling a python and getting into a shootout among leaking wine barrels. This character, who was created by a pair of authors working as automotive journalists, robs a Russian princess of her pocketbook and jewels since she was too embarrassed to get out of the bath. In the first book titled for this character, he hacks a Marquise to death in her chateau and stuffs the body of an English Lord into a chest. This character’s daughter (*) Hélène is a love interest of the journalist Fandor, who assists Inspector Juve with tracking this character. Marcel Allain and Pierre Souvestre created this character, who appeared in 43 pulp novels and a series of crime films by Louis Feuillade. For 10 points, name this mask- and tophat-wearing master criminal from the popular fiction of Belle Epoque France.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one novel, this character travels to South Africa, assassinates his daughter's elderly guardian, organizes the lynching of the Black man Jupiter, and then releases hundreds of plague-ridden rats on a ship. John Ashbury's introduction to a Penguin Classic about this character notes memorable scenes involving him battling a python and getting into a shootout among leaking wine barrels. This character, who was created by a pair of authors working as automotive journalists, robs a Russian princess of her pocketbook and jewels since she was too embarrassed to get out of the bath. In the first book titled for this character, he hacks a Marquise to death in her chateau and stuffs the body of an English Lord into a chest. This character's daughter (*) Helene is a love interest of the journalist Fandor, who assists Inspector Juve with tracking this character. Marcel Allain and Pierre Souvestre created this character, who appeared in 43 pulp novels and a series of crime films by Louis Feuillade. For 10 points, name this mask- and tophat-wearing master criminal from the popular fiction of Belle Epoque France.

bonuses.leadin: This character breathes through a punctured wine bottle while hiding in a basement cistern near the end of a film titled for his battle of wits with his nemesis Inspector Juve. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this criminal mastermind, the star of five internationally-popular French silent films directed by Louis Feuillade from 1913-14.

A fan club called the Society of the Friends of Fantômas was co-founded by two early proponents of this artistic movement. A razor slices a woman’s eyeball in a seminal film from this movement, Un Chien Andalou.

This director drew from Juve contre Fantômas to create a sequence in which the title character shuffles a deck of cards to choose a disguise to perpetrate a stock market panic, in the opening sequence of his first film about the criminal psychologist Dr. Mabuse.
answers: Surrealism [the Society of the Friends of Fantômas was founded by Guillaume Apollinaire and Max Jacob]
answers_sanitized: Surrealism [the Society of the Friends of Fantomas was founded by Guillaume Apollinaire and Max Jacob]
Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang]
